name: Hamburg-Style Fish Salad
recipeInstructions:
Fillets may be fresh or frozen. They can include cod, turbot, or other white fish.
1. Melt butter in a frying pan over medium heat. Place the fish fillets in the pan and pour hot water over them until half of the fillets are covered. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer gently for 10 minutes, ensuring the fillets remain tender but not overcooked.
2. Slice the hard-cooked eggs and pickles into thin rounds. Drain excess moisture from the fish by patting it dry with paper towels.
3. Combine the fish cubes with egg slices, pickle pieces, and capers in a large bowl.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, sour cream, lemon juice, mustard, salt, and pepper to create the salad sauce.
5. Gently mix the prepared salad sauce into the fish mixture.
6. Arrange dollops of fish mixture in individual serving dishes. Spoon the salad sauce over the top of each serving.
7. Refrigerate for 30 minutes before serving.
8. Garnish by cutting the remaining hard-cooked egg into eight pieces and arranging on top of each dish. Add chopped beet slices as desired. Serve immediately.
